result:
I'd make it so that every human being is born with an overwhelming, almost compulsive curiosity about how reality actually works—strong enough to override tribalism, dogma, ego, and short-term comfort.

Most of the world's worst problems (war, corruption, bad decisions, self-deception) aren't caused by lack of intelligence or resources. They're caused by people not really wanting to know the truth when it threatens their identity, status, or narrative. If that changed at the root, almost everything else would follow.

message.content:
I'd make it so that every human being is born with an overwhelming, almost compulsive curiosity about how reality actually works—strong enough to override tribalism, dogma, ego, and short-term comfort.

Most of the world's worst problems (war, corruption, bad decisions, self-deception) aren't caused by lack of intelligence or resources. They're caused by people not really wanting to know the truth when it threatens their identity, status, or narrative. If that changed at the root, almost everything else would follow.
message.reasoning_content: If I could change the world in one way, I'd make sure every person has an unshakeable drive to seek truth, no matter how tough it might be. That way, decisions get based more on reality than on biases or tribal loyalties, and we could all push forward together in understanding the universe.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
